source: TBPN — SpaceX IPO, token path, sovereign AI won't reach frontier
summary: Retail investors have been a more powerful market force over the last 3.5 years than at any point including the 2000 bubble, and are likely outperforming most professional managers
summary_extended: Baker argues that retail investors have been the most powerful market force over the past 3.5 years, surpassing even the retail frenzy seen during the 2000 dot-com bubble in terms of sustained influence. He supports this claim by pointing to retail favorites indices that have risen significantly in both 2024 and 2025, suggesting retail investors as a cohort are likely outperforming the overwhelming majority of professional money managers across private equity, venture capital, and public equity. Baker cautions against the reflexive professional tendency to dismiss retail participation as a contrarian or negative signal, arguing instead that retail is a durable structural force in markets. The thesis carries no specific forward-looking buy or sell recommendation, but implies that strategies or vehicles aligned with retail investor preferences may continue to outperform institutionally managed alternatives.
detail: Baker argues retail should not be dismissed pejoratively — retail favorites indices are up significantly in 2024 and 2025, likely outperforming the majority of professional money managers across PE, VC, and public equity. Retail is a structural force in markets, not a contrarian indicator.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as an observation with supporting data (retail indices performance) but doesn't make a specific forward-looking investment call
quote_fragment: retail is probably outperforming the overwhelming majority of professional money managers
